title: Coast Guard Improvement Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill creates a new top leader for the Coast Guard called the Secretary of the Coast Guard. The President will choose this person and the Senate must approve them. Right now, the Coast Guard Commandant reports directly to the Secretary of Homeland Security, but under this new law, they would report to the new Secretary of the Coast Guard instead. This new Secretary would be in charge of running all Coast Guard operations.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Coast Guard Improvement Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Secretary of the Coast Guard
sections.text: This section creates a new top leader for the Coast Guard called the Secretary of the Coast Guard. The President will choose this person and the Senate must approve the choice. This person must be a civilian, not someone currently serving in the military. No one can be picked for this job if they were an active military officer in the past five years. The Secretary of the Coast Guard will be in charge of all Coast Guard activities including recruiting new members, training them, buying equipment, and managing buildings and facilities. This person will report directly to the Secretary of Homeland Security when the Coast Guard works under that department. When the Coast Guard works with the Navy during wartime, this person can give advice to the Navy Secretary. The current Coast Guard leader called the Commandant will now report to this new Secretary of the Coast Guard instead of directly to the Homeland Security Secretary. The Commandant will help the Secretary of the Coast Guard run day to day operations and carry out approved plans. Within thirty days of this law taking effect, the Homeland Security Secretary must give Congress a detailed plan showing how many people will work in the new Secretary of the Coast Guard office and how the department will be reorganized. This plan must explain what responsibilities will move from the Commandant to the new Secretary, what property and money will be transferred, and when these changes will happen. Congress can ask for changes to this plan before it goes into effect.
